I found comprehensive UX research and best practices on card UI design, information hierarchy, CTA placement, and readability in high-density grid layouts. Here are the key findings:

## Information Hierarchy

Strong visual hierarchy guides attention using size, spacing, contrast, and consistent alignment. Use different font sizes and weights to establish a clear visual hierarchy, typically using a larger font for the title and smaller fonts for body text. Incorporating visual hierarchy, iconography, and color schemes within cards also aids readability, helping users navigate complex information more intuitively.

## Card Structure & Content Organization

The card body can contain: a title; a subtitle; supporting text/description; and, labels/badges. The footer serves as a primary action area for elements like buttons, CTAs, or other controls. Keep content snapshot-like: one hero image, short text, and minimal metadata to avoid overload.

## CTA Placement

Position primary CTAs in the bottom third for easy thumb access. Multiple CTAs can confuse users and reduce interaction clarity. If you need secondary actions, make them visually subordinate to the primary CTA.

## High-Density Grid Readability

Aim for similar card sizes and content length within groups or carousels. Maintain even padding inside the card (both vertically and horizontally). Avoid overcrowding and add enough space between the edges and the content. Clarity always beats density.

Opt for left alignment to optimize readability and avoid centering text and content.
